description: Unified Real-Time Event Pipeline - OTEL ingestion, Codex notify hooks, expanded Claude hook coverage, and pipeline hardening

userStories.title: Define NormalizedEvent types and EventBus module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a unified event model and broadcast channel so all event sources (OTEL, hooks, Codex notify, polling) produce the same type and downstream consumers are source-agnostic.
acceptanceCriteria: Add NormalizedEvent enum to src-tauri/src/models.rs with variants: SessionStarted, SessionEnded, ToolStarted, ToolCompleted, StatusChanged, TokensUsed, PermissionRequested, TaskCompleted, Error
Each variant carries common fields: agent_type (AgentType), session_id (String), timestamp (String), source (EventSource enum: Otel, Hook, Notify, Poll)

Variant-specific fields: ToolStarted has tool_name/tool_input, ToolCompleted adds status/duration_ms/result_preview, StatusChanged has new_status (AgentStatus), TokensUsed has input_tokens/output_tokens, PermissionRequested has tool_name/tool_input/request_id
Create src-tauri/src/event_bus.rs with EventBus struct wrapping tokio::sync::broadcast::Sender<NormalizedEvent>
EventBus has publish(event: NormalizedEvent) and subscribe() -> broadcast::Receiver<NormalizedEvent> methods
EventBus is Clone-friendly (broadcast::Sender is Clone) for passing to multiple producers
Add NormalizedEvent and EventSource types to src/types.ts matching the Rust definitions
cargo check passes

userStories.title: Create OTEL HTTP/protobuf ingestion server
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an OTLP/HTTP server on localhost:4318 that accepts protobuf-encoded trace exports so AI agents can push telemetry directly.
acceptanceCriteria: Add dependencies to Cargo.toml: opentelemetry-proto (with 'gen-tonic' or appropriate feature for trace types), prost, axum, tower-http
Create src-tauri/src/otel_server.rs with an async start() function that binds axum to 127.0.0.1:4318
Implement POST /v1/traces handler that accepts Content-Type application/x-protobuf
Decode request body as opentelemetry_proto::tonic::collector::trace::v1::ExportTraceServiceRequest using prost::Message::decode
Log received spans at debug level (span name, service.name, trace_id) for development visibility
Return ExportTraceServiceResponse (empty, 200 OK) on success, 400 on decode failure
Start OTEL server as a tokio task in lib.rs setup block, alongside existing hook server
Server gracefully handles port-already-in-use (log warning, skip OTEL server, app still works)
cargo check passes

userStories.title: Map OTEL spans to NormalizedEvent and wire EventBus
userStories.description: As a developer, I want incoming OTEL spans mapped to NormalizedEvent and published to the EventBus, with a Tauri event bridge so the frontend receives them.
acceptanceCriteria: Create span-to-event mapping in otel_server.rs: extract agent_type from service.name or resource attributes, session_id from span attributes, tool_name from span name/attributes
Map span names containing 'tool' or 'function_call' to ToolStarted/ToolCompleted based on span status
Map session lifecycle spans to SessionStarted/SessionEnded
Map token/usage spans to TokensUsed (extract input_tokens/output_tokens from attributes)
Unknown/unmapped span types logged at debug level and skipped (no crash or error)
OTEL server takes EventBus reference and publishes mapped NormalizedEvent for each span
Create EventBus Tauri bridge: spawn a tokio task that subscribes to EventBus and emits 'event-bus:normalized-event' Tauri event for each NormalizedEvent
EventBus initialized in lib.rs setup, passed to OTEL server and bridge task
cargo check passes

userStories.title: Integrate hook server with EventBus
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the existing hook server to publish NormalizedEvent to the EventBus so hooks and OTEL events flow through the same pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: HookServer::start() takes an EventBus parameter in addition to AppHandle
Non-permission hook events (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, UserPromptSubmit, Stop, etc.) publish corresponding NormalizedEvent to the EventBus
PreToolUse maps to ToolStarted, PostToolUse to ToolCompleted, Stop/SubagentStop/SessionEnd to SessionEnded, SessionStart to SessionStarted, UserPromptSubmit to StatusChanged(Operating)
Existing Tauri event emissions (hook:status-update, hook:permission-request, hook:permission-cancelled) remain unchanged for backwards compatibility
PermissionRequest events still use the existing direct Tauri event path (not EventBus) since they require the blocking approval round-trip
Update lib.rs to pass EventBus to HookServer::start()
cargo check passes

userStories.title: Add frontend event bus consumer with OTEL status merging
userStories.description: As a user, I want sessions that report via OTEL to show real-time status in the notchai UI, with OTEL and hook events taking priority over polled status.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/hooks/useEventBus.ts that listens for 'event-bus:normalized-event' Tauri events
Maintains Map<sessionId, { status: AgentStatus, timestamp: number, source: EventSource }> for latest event per session
Maps NormalizedEvent variants to AgentStatus: ToolStarted/StatusChanged(Operating) -> operating, SessionEnded/TaskCompleted -> completed, ToolCompleted -> operating (still working)
Export mergeEventBusStatus() function that applies event-bus-sourced status when event is < 30 seconds old (same TTL as existing hook merge)
Integrate into useAgentMonitor.ts: call mergeEventBusStatus() after existing mergeHookStatus(), so priority is event-bus > hooks > poll
Falls back gracefully when no event-bus events received (existing behavior unchanged)
npx tsc --noEmit passes

userStories.title: Create Codex notify relay script
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a lightweight script that Codex's notify command invokes to relay turn-completion data to notchai.
acceptanceCriteria: Create resources/notchai-codex-notify.sh as a bash script

Script sends a JSON payload to /tmp/notchai.sock with fields: source ('codex'), event_type ('task_complete'), session_id (from CODEX_SESSION_ID env var or 'unknown'), cwd (from pwd), timestamp (ISO 8601)
Uses socat or python one-liner fallback to write to Unix socket (socat preferred, python3 fallback)
Exits 0 on any error (fail-open, never blocks Codex)
Script is executable (chmod 755)
Add resources/notchai-codex-notify.sh to bundle resources in tauri.conf.json
cargo check passes

userStories.title: Create Codex notify hook installer
userStories.description: As a user, I want notchai to install a Codex notify command so Codex pushes turn-completion events to notchai in near-real-time.
acceptanceCriteria: Add install_codex_hooks() and uninstall_codex_hooks() functions to hook_installer.rs (or new codex_hook_installer.rs)
Reads ~/.codex/config.yaml (or config.toml), adds notify.command entry pointing to bundled notchai-codex-notify.sh
Installation is idempotent — checks for 'notchai' marker in existing notify config before adding
Uninstall removes the notify entry cleanly without affecting other config keys
install_codex_hooks_if_enabled() checks ~/.notchai/config.json for codex_hooks_enabled flag (default true)
Hook server extended to recognize messages with source='codex' and publish NormalizedEvent::TaskCompleted or StatusChanged to EventBus
Wire install into lib.rs setup block alongside Claude hooks
Add codex_hooks_enabled toggle to existing toggle_hooks_enabled Tauri command (or add separate toggle_codex_hooks_enabled command)
cargo check passes

userStories.title: Add Codex hooks toggle to settings UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want a toggle in the settings panel to enable/disable Codex notify hooks independently from Claude hooks.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Codex hooks toggle in ExpandedView settings area, alongside existing Claude hooks toggle
Toggle calls invoke('toggle_codex_hooks_enabled', { enabled }) or the unified toggle command
Shows current state fetched on mount via Tauri command
Label clearly indicates 'Codex Hooks' vs existing 'Claude Hooks' toggle
State persists via ~/.notchai/config.json codex_hooks_enabled flag
npx tsc --noEmit passes

userStories.title: Expand Claude hook event coverage
userStories.description: As a user, I want notchai to handle additional Claude Code hook events for richer session status and multi-agent awareness.
acceptanceCriteria: Review current Claude Code hook event types and add any new ones not yet registered in hook_installer.rs (e.g., if Claude Code has added new hook event types beyond the current 9)
hook_server.rs maps Notification events with title/message to NormalizedEvent and triggers macOS notification via Tauri notification API
hook_server.rs handles unknown/new event types gracefully — logs at debug level and publishes a generic StatusChanged event
useHookEvents.ts status mapping updated: add handling for any new event types in the hookEventType-to-status switch
Frontend shows notification content in collapsed view when a Notification event arrives (brief flash of title text)
npx tsc --noEmit passes
cargo check passes

userStories.title: Add approval pipeline dedup and TTL
userStories.description: As a user, I don't want to see duplicate or stale permission requests cluttering the approval UI.
acceptanceCriteria: hook_server.rs deduplicates pending approvals by (session_id, tool_name, tool_use_id) — if an identical request arrives while one is pending, the old oneshot sender is dropped (cancelled) and replaced
Pending approvals expire after 5 minutes — a tokio::time::sleep branch in the select! macro sends a deny response and removes from pending map
When a pending approval is cancelled (old deduped or expired), emit hook:permission-cancelled Tauri event so frontend removes it
Frontend useHookEvents.ts filters pendingApprovals on each update to remove entries older than 5 minutes as a safety net
Add APPROVAL_TTL_SECS constant (300) to hook_server.rs for configurability
npx tsc --noEmit passes
cargo check passes

userStories.title: Add incremental reads for Codex and Gemini adapters
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Codex and Gemini adapters to use incremental file reading instead of reading entire files on each poll cycle.
acceptanceCriteria: Codex adapter tracks byte offsets per session file in a HashMap<String, u64> (similar to TranscriptReader in transcript.rs)
On first read, Codex adapter reads last 50KB of JSONL file; on subsequent reads, seeks to last known offset
Gemini adapter tracks file modification time per session in HashMap<String, SystemTime>
Gemini adapter skips re-parsing files whose mtime has not changed since last poll
Both adapters accumulate session state (tokens, status, message count) incrementally instead of recomputing from scratch
No change in output — sessions and tool calls returned are identical to current full-read behavior
cargo check passes

userStories.title: Add parser and adapter unit tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want unit tests for transcript parsing, OTEL span mapping, and adapter session extraction to prevent regressions.
acceptanceCriteria: Unit tests for TranscriptReader: normal conversation (2+ messages), tool use + tool result correlation, malformed/truncated JSONL line
Unit tests for OTEL span-to-NormalizedEvent mapping: valid tool span, session lifecycle span, unknown span type (should not panic)
Unit tests for Codex adapter JSONL parsing: session_meta entry, event_msg with user_message/agent_message/token_count subtypes
Unit tests for Gemini adapter JSON parsing: conversation record with messages, tool_calls extraction from assistant message
Unit test for NormalizedEvent serde serialization round-trip (serialize then deserialize, assert equal)
All tests pass with cargo test
